title: Most Probable Number (MPN) Counts of Cyanophages

steps: Label the dilution preparation plate using example in guidelines The numbers “-1” through “-7” represent dilution levels, i.e. 10 -1 through 10 -7 -fold dilutions; “0” indicates no phage.
Label the sample plates with the date, host, phage name, and any other applicable information (e.g. the light level) The numbering of the rows and columns must be the same as the dilution plate.
Using a 8x1500 µL multi-channel pipettor, put 270 µL of Pro99 or SN medium in all wells of dilution plate Will require ~30 mL of medium in a sterile plastic trough.
Using a 100 µL pipettor, dispense 30 µL of virus sample into row H of the dilution plate This will be the 10 -1 dilution of virus. Each virus sample is designated two wells per row.
Repeat this for each of the virus samples
Using a 12x300 µL multi-channel pipettor set at 30 µL with 12 tips attached, pipette row H up and down to mix the dilution
Replace the tips and pipette 30 µL from row H into row A (the 10 -2 row)
Pipette up and down several times to mix
Replace the tips and transfer 30 µL into row B (10 -3 row)

Repeat this mixing, replacing tips and transferring 4 more times until you are at row F (10 -7 row) TIP: using the lid to cover the rows that have not yet had virus put in them helps to prevent cross-contamination of the rows. Do not transfer anything to row G since this is the ”no phage” row.

Using a 12x300 µL multi-channel pipettor set to 30 µL with 8 tips attached, pipet 30 µL host cells into all appropriate wells of the sample plate These are all the wells in columns 2 through 11 (the first and last column of each plate contain medium that provides moisture so that the test wells do not evaporate during incubation).

Reset the pipettor to dispense 20 µL, put on 8 fresh tips and transfer 20 µL from the virus dilution plate to the same corresponding column in the sample test plate You should replace the tips with fresh ones for each column. If you are testing several different hosts, you can set the pipettor to step up to 10 times at 20 µL and inoculate up to 10 different hosts, each in a separate test plate.
Put the lids on the sample test plates and place in incubator under appropriate light and temperature conditions for each host, and let the virus adsorb to the host for 1 hr 01:00:00

Using the 8x1500 µL multi-channel pipettor set to step 6x200 µL, add 200 µL of Pro99 or SN medium to all wells in all sample plates This will come to 250 µL per well and requires 20 mL per sample test plate.

Seal each plate with parafilm and return plates to the incubator Plates may be read in 1-2 weeks, however, it might be preferable to get an initial reading on the plates and monitor progress every 2-3 days. We use the Appliskan plate reader, set to read fluorescent emission using a 485 µm excitation filter and a 675 µm (for green fluorescence) or 590 µm (for red fluorescence) emission filter.
creator: Verve Team
